kill_fact: The catalyst is GENUINELY LIVE and the load-bearing facts survive primary-source verification: signed merger ($155.00 cash + 0.7720 CTAS), shareholder vote PASSED 06-12-2026 (>99% of votes cast, ~95% of shares) per UNF 8-K, and the ONLY open gate (FTC antitrust) is real and unresolved — the Second Request was issued 06-11-2026 to BOTH parties, extending the HSR period until 30 days after substantial compliance (CTAS 8-K). The $350M reverse-termination fee and Jan-10-2027 outside date (+up to 8mo extensions) are confirmed in the merger agreement. BUT the dossier's asymmetry is STALE on price: live UNF is ~$276 (current quote), not ~$263. At UNF $276 / CTAS ~$170 the deal value ~$287 leaves upside of only ~+$11 (+4%), while a hard-block floor ~$222 is -$54 (-20%) — a far WORSE risk/reward (~0.2:1 raw) than the dossier's +9%/-16% claim. The spread has already substantially collapsed; the easy carry is gone.
corrections: Live UNF price is ~$276 (not $263) — upside to deal value (~$287) is ~+$11/+4%, NOT +$24/+9%; downside to the ~$222 antitrust-block floor is ~-$54/-20%. Raw outcome ratio is ~0.2:1, materially worse than the dossier's 0.6:1. Probability-weighted EV (~$272-276 at 78/22 odds) now sits essentially AT the live price, not comfortably above it — the positive-carry claim is no longer load-bearing. The FTC Second Request on a #1+#2 horizontal in US uniform rental is a genuine block risk the bull may under-weight at 22%.
evidence: UNF 8-K / press release (06-12-2026): UniFirst shareholders approved the Cintas merger, >99% of votes cast in favor (~95% of all common + Class B shares); terms $155.00 cash + 0.7720 CTAS/share; close expected H2 calendar 2026 — sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0000717954/000114036126025000/ef20075767_ex99-1.htm

CTAS 8-K (filed re 06-11-2026): 'On June 11, 2026, each of Cintas and UniFirst received a request for additional information (the Second Request)' from the FTC; HSR waiting period extends until 30 days after both parties substantially comply; Cintas continues to expect close in H2 calendar 2026 — sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0000723254/000095010326008890/dp248355_8k.htm

Merger agreement / DEFM14A: $350M reverse termination fee payable by Cintas on antitrust failure; UniFirst $213.3M company termination fee; outside/End Date Jan-10-2027 with automatic extensions up to two 4-month periods — sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0000717954/000114036126020380/ny20072400x1_defm14a.htm

Live quotes (06-2026): UNF ~$275-276, CTAS ~$169-170 → live deal value ~$155 + 0.7720x170 = ~$286; gross spread to $276 is only ~$11/+4%, sharply narrower than the dossier's ~$24/8.5% — stockanalysis.com/stocks/unf/
